contexts.text:
Why can't I assign my resource to a new resource group? 

You can't reassign an IBM Cloud® resource to a different resource group after it has already been created.

  What’s happening 

You don't have the option to move a resource from one resource group to another.

  Why it’s happening 

Resources can't be moved after they are assigned to a resource group.

  How to fix it 

To correct this issue, you can try to delete the resource and create a new instance of it from the catalog. This way, you can be sure to assign the new instance to the correct resource group.

Does deleting a feature flag or property in an environment delete it across all environments? 

  What’s happening 

Deleting a feature flag or property in an environment deletes it for all environments.

  Why it’s happening 

Feature flag or property is available to all environments. Updates to values are per environment. Create and delete action executes across all environments.

  How to fix it 

Deletion of feature flag or property is across all environments.

If a flag is not required in a particular environment, disable the flag or set the values default. If a flag is not required in any environment, it can be deleted.

Why is my IBMid or email not recognized when I try to log in to the console? 

To successfully log in to the IBM Cloud® console, make sure that you have IBMid authentication for each account.

  What’s happening 

When you log in to the console, the following message is displayed:

> We didn't recognize this IBMid or email.

  Why it’s happening 

You tried to log in to the console, but didn't use a valid IBMid. For example, you didn't enter a fully qualified email address for the IBMid, or you tried to use a previous user name and password.

  How to fix it 

You must have a valid IBMid and password to log in to IBM Cloud. Enter a fully qualified email address for the IBMid.

Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.users.create Create pre-registered user profile. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.users.get Export user profiles. Must be done through the API. 
 appid.refresh-token.revoke Revoke all the refresh tokens that are issued for the user. Must be done through the API. 
 appid.users.import Import user profiles. Must be done through the API. 
 appid.user-profile.read View a user profile. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.user-profile.update Update a user profile. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.user-profile.bulkdelete Delete a list of user profiles. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.user-roles.read View the user roles. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.user-roles.update Update the user roles. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > User Profiles tab. 
 appid.roles.read View the roles list. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > Roles tab. 
 appid.roles.create Create a role. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > Roles tab. 
 appid.role.read View the role. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > Roles tab. 
 appid.role.update Update the role. Can be found in the Profiles and roles > Roles tab. 
 appid.applications.read View the applications list. Can be found in the Applications tab. 
 appid.applications.create Create an application. Can be found in the Applications tab. 
 appid.application.read View the application. Can be found in the Applications tab. 
 appid.application.update Update the application. Can be found in the Applications tab. 
 appid.application-scopes.read View the application scopes. Can be found in the Applications tab.
